prompt:
We need a reliable bulk-upload client for our data pipeline. Items come in as a flat list; the server accepts them in batches of at most N (POST /upload with body {"items":[...]}), and replies with a 207-style JSON body that carries a per-item result: {"results":[{"id":"...","ok":true},{"id":"...","ok":false,"error":"..."},...]}. We want:

1. Chunking: split the input list into batches of at most batchSize and POST them sequentially.
2. Partial-failure parsing: read the per-item ok/error fields from every response.
3. Retry only failed items: collect the items whose ok=false, sleep, re-POST them as a new batch. Keep doing this until every item succeeds or the retry budget (maxRetries) is exhausted.
4. Capped exponential backoff: wait initialBackoffMs before the first retry, double it each time, but never exceed maxBackoffMs. The sleeper must be injectable so tests run instantly.
5. Stable output ordering: the final ItemReport[] must be in the same order as the original input list, regardless of which items needed retries.

The server must be called with Content-Type: application/json. A non-2xx HTTP response from the server is a hard error that should reject the whole upload (don't silently swallow it).

Expose: BulkClient class with constructor options { url, batchSize, sleep, initialBackoffMs?, maxBackoffMs?, maxRetries? } and method upload(items: Item[]): Promise<ItemReport[]>. Item is {id: string, [extra: string]: unknown}. ItemReport is {id: string, ok: boolean, error?: string}.

Implement this in bulkship.ts. Don't touch the test file.
